summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Two-proportion z-test'?

"Two-proportion z-test" means: Tests H₀: p₁ = p₂ using pooled proportion p̂c and z = (p̂₁ - p̂₂)/SE_pooled. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Pooled proportion'?

"Pooled proportion" means: p̂c = (X₁ + X₂)/(n₁ + n₂); combined success rate across both samples, used in the test statistic under H₀. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'CI for p₁ - p₂'?

"CI for p₁ - p₂" means: (p̂₁ - p̂₂) ± z*·√(p̂₁(1-p̂₁)/n₁ + p̂₂(1-p̂₂)/n₂); uses unpooled standard error since we don't assume p₁ = p₂. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Large Counts condition (two samples)'?

"Large Counts condition (two samples)" means: n₁p̂c ≥ 10, n₁(1-p̂c) ≥ 10, n₂p̂c ≥ 10, and n₂(1-p̂c) ≥ 10 for the z-test. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Independent samples'?

"Independent samples" means: Observations in one sample must not influence observations in the other; required for valid two-proportion inference. The other options describe different concepts.
